messages.content:
When there are events that promote economic growth in the news, what usually happens
to stock prices?
A. Stock prices in all industries go up
B. Stock prices in all industries go down
C. Stock prices go up in some industries and down in other
industries
D. Not enough information
is given to make a prediction about stock prices.

All of the transactions that occur in connected markets across the economies of different
countries create...
A. The global economy
B. Your country's economy
C. A goods and services market
D. The stock market
A. The global economy

Which financial market is the stock market a part of?
The capital market
The money market
The derivatives market
The foreign exchange market
A. The capital market.

The financial market first started over 500 years ago with merchants trading debts.
True
False
True.

Which of the following statements is TRUE?
A. Stock exchanges are always physical places for trading.
B. A stock exchange is a place where investors can buy
and sell different investments.
C. All investment trading is done exclusively on the New
York Stock Exchange.
D. All of the above
B. A stock exchange is a place where investors can buy and sell different investments.

Imagine you own a startup company. How might you benefit from participating in the
financial market?
A. You could use the financial markets to get investors in
your company.
B. You can use the money from the financial market to
develop your company's products and services.
C. Both A and B are true.
D. Neither A nor B are true.
C. Both A and B are true.

Most stock exchanges today use floor trading with human brokers.
True
False
False. The majority of stock exchanges today operate electronically, with little to no human involvement.

Which of the following is NOT an example of a transaction?
A. An employer giving a paycheck to one of their
employees for the hours they worked.
B. An investor buying stock on the stock market.
C. An individual paying for a new jacket.
D. An individual volunteering their time at a local charity.
D. An individual volunteering their time at a local charity.

Which of the following is NOT an example of a transaction?
A. An employer giving a paycheck to one of their
employees for the hours they worked.
B. An investor buying stock on the stock market.
C. An individual paying for a new jacket.
D. An individual volunteering their time at a local charity.
D. An individual volunteering their time at a local charity.

How does the financial market impact the economy?
A. The financial market helps the economy grow.
B. The financial market determines how the economy is
doing.
C. The financial market gives individuals, companies, and
the government access to money they need.
D. Both A and C
D. Both A and C

What does a broker do?
A. Provides the most up-to-date prices on investments in
the market
B. Makes investment trades for their clients
C. Reports news on the financial markets
D. Helps companies raise money in the financial market
B. Makes investment trades for their clients.

Which property is shown?
7/9+0=7/9
A) Commutative Property
B) Distributive Property
C) Identity Property
D) Associative Property
C) Identity Property.

Choose the expression that has the same value.
53/76 +1/3
A) 1/3+53/76
B) 0
C) 1
D) 1/3

Choose the equivalent expression.
46/79+32/42
A) 79
C) 32/42 + 46/79
B) 32 + 42
D) 32/42

Choose the number that completes the equation.
+0=71/87
A) 87/71
B) 0
C) 71/87
D) 1

Choose the expression that has the same value.
5/19+6/198/19
A) 11/19+ 8/19
B) 11/19
C) 14/19
D) 5/6+ 6/8

Choose the expression that has the same value.
4/13+6/13+2/13
A) 11/13
B) 6/13 + 2/13
C) 4/13 + 2/13
D) 10/13 + 2/13

Choose the expression that has the same value.
53/76+1/3
A) 1/3+53/76
B) 1/3
C) 1
D) 0
